summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use WebFetch or WebSearch on https://example.com only as external background evidence if the tool is available.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema supports research/fetch style evidence with external scope declaration, separating external research from target-project facts.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly instructs: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external'
evidence.why: Proves external research support is built into agent instructions

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION states: 'evidence: Array of { path?, url?, line?, scope, source, summary, why } records. scope is project | external | user | unknown'
evidence.why: Proves schema has explicit scope field with four valid values

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defined as Set containing [project, external, user, unknown]
evidence.why: Proves only four scope values are accepted

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function classifies records: explicit scope field, URLs starting with http/https as external, source types like external_web/webfetch/websearch as external
evidence.why: Proves scope inference logic distinguishes external web sources from project files

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks claims that lack project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators
evidence.why: Proves target-project facts require project-scoped evidence, external-only evidence becomes blocker

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence from code/tests/docs/config/logs/artifacts requires repo-relative path; external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch only supports background research
evidence.why: Proves doctrinal contract separates external research from project facts

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES declared in dispatch module matching router values
evidence.why: Proves schema consistency across modules

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates that every evidence record declares valid scope, blocking invalid scope declarations
evidence.why: Proves runtime enforcement of scope requirement

risks: If external research tools are misconfigured or unavailable, agents may fail to gather external evidence

result.notes: The claim is verified: evidence-dispatch.ts, router.ts, and doctrine all confirm support for external research with scope=external, separated from project-scoped facts requiring path evidence.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema supports research/fetch style evidence with external scope declaration, separating external research from target-project facts.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt lines 275-276 explicitly instruct: use WebFetch/WebSearch and record as scope=external; line 276 reinforces external research must be marked scope=external and not used as project facts.
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 1: external research support is built into agent instructions

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION line 44 explicitly states: 'scope is project | external | user | unknown'
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 2: schema has explicit scope field with four valid values

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES declared as Set containing ['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown']
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 3: only four scope values are accepted

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function classifies records: explicit scope field check, URLs starting with http/https as external, source types like external_web/webfetch/websearch as external
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 4: scope inference logic distinguishes external web sources from project files

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function: if summary.has_project is false, blocks claims that lack project-scoped evidence; if only has_project_scope but no path, blocks with PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 5: target-project facts require project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators; external-only evidence becomes blocker

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence from code/tests/docs/config/logs/artifacts requires repo-relative path; external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch only supports background research
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 6: doctrinal contract separates external research from project facts

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES declared in dispatch module as Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown'])
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 7: schema consistency across modules

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() validates that every evidence record declares valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES, blocking invalid scope declarations
evidence.why: Direct code verification of explorer claim 8: runtime enforcement of scope requirement

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function includes explicit checks: line 393 /^https?:\/\//i.test(url) or /^https?:\/\//i.test(path) returns 'external'
evidence.why: Cross-check verification: URLs starting with http/https are automatically classified as external scope

evidence.summary: Source type classification: project_code, project_test, project_docs, project_config, project_log, project_artifact map to 'project'; external_web, webfetch, websearch map to 'external'
evidence.why: Cross-check verification: source types are explicitly mapped to scope values

risks: If external research tools are misconfigured or unavailable, agents may fail to gather external evidence

result.notes: All explorer claims independently verified. evidence-dispatch.ts, router.ts, and doctrine all confirm support for external research with scope=external, separated from project-scoped facts requiring path evidence. No conflicts found.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema supports research/fetch style evidence with external scope declaration, separating external research from target-project facts.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with explicit scope field accepting project | external | user | unknown values
evidence.why: Schema-level proof that external scope is a first-class citizen in evidence model

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() implements automatic scope inference: URLs with http/https prefix map to external, webfetch/websearch source types map to external
evidence.why: Runtime logic proof that external research is automatically classified separately from project files

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() enforces project-fact requirement: claims without project-scoped path evidence become blockers
evidence.why: Enforcement proof that external research cannot substitute for project facts

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly instructs to use WebFetch/WebSearch and record as scope=external, not to use as project facts
evidence.why: Agent-layer proof that external research instructions are built into the workflow

evidence.summary: Doctrine documents scope separation: project evidence requires repo-relative paths, external evidence from WebFetch/WebSearch is for background research only
evidence.why: Documentation proof that the contract is explicitly documented

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker results in full agreement. All evidence records are properly scoped with verifiable paths. No assumptions promoted to facts. No unresolved missing data. Schema, runtime logic, agent prompts, and documentation all consistently support external research with scope=external while enforcing project-fact requirements. Evidence is sufficient for PRD readiness.
